prompt: This code throws IllegalStateException: stream has already been operated upon or closed. What is the bug?
choices: filter() and count() cannot be chained on the same stream
A stream can only be consumed once — once a terminal operation (count) runs, the stream is closed and cannot be reused
mapToLong requires an IntStream, not a Stream<Integer>
nums.stream() must be reassigned after each terminal operation
explanation: Streams are single-use pipelines. Once a terminal operation (count(), collect(), sum(), etc.) is called, the stream is consumed and closed. Any further operation on the same stream reference throws IllegalStateException. Fix: call nums.stream() again for the second pipeline — Stream<Integer> s2 = nums.stream();
